It describes the design and implementation of a novel 'Optimal Deep Neural Network Driven Computer Aided Diagnosis Model for Skin Cancer Detection and Classification (ODNN–CADSCC)', detailing specific methodologies (Wiener Filtering, U-Net, SqueezeNet, IWOA) and reporting original experimental results (99.90% accuracy) rather than summarizing prior literature.

title: Optimal deep neural network-driven computer aided diagnosis model for skin cancer
brief_summary: This study proposes an Optimal Deep Neural Network Driven Computer Aided Diagnosis Model for Skin Cancer Detection and Classification (ODNN–CADSCC) to address the need for accurate skin cancer classification using deep learning. The methodology employs Wiener Filtering for preprocessing, U-Net for image segmentation, and SqueezeNet for feature extraction from dermoscopic images. An Improved Whale Optimization Algorithm (IWOA) is utilized to optimize the parameters of a Deep Neural Network (DNN) for the final classification task. The model's performance was evaluated through comparative analysis against recent approaches. The results demonstrated that the proposed ODNN–CADSCC model achieved a maximum accuracy of 99.90%, establishing its promising performance over existing methods.

lesion_types_studied.details: The paper focuses on the detection and classification of skin cancer using dermoscopic images. The specific histological subtypes (e.g., melanoma, BCC, SCC) are not explicitly listed in the provided context, which refers generally to 'skin cancer' as the diagnostic category.

all_task_types.details: The paper explicitly states the goal is 'skin cancer detection and classification' using an ODNN-CADSCC model. It employs SqueezeNet for feature extraction and an Improved Whale Optimization Algorithm (IWOA) with a Deep Neural Network (DNN) for the classification task, achieving 99.90% accuracy. While U-Net is used for segmentation, it serves as a preprocessing step for the primary classification task.

conclusions: The proposed Optimal Deep Neural Network Driven Computer Aided Diagnosis Model for Skin Cancer Detection and Classification (ODNN–CADSCC) demonstrates promising performance compared to recent approaches.
The ODNN–CADSCC model achieved a maximum accuracy of 99.90% in skin cancer detection and classification.

The integration of Wiener Filtering for preprocessing, U-Net for segmentation, SqueezeNet for feature extraction, and an Improved Whale Optimization Algorithm (IWOA) with a Deep Neural Network (DNN) for classification is effective for accurate skin cancer classification.
important_remarks: The specific dataset used for training and testing is not explicitly named in the provided context, raising concerns about reproducibility and potential dataset bias.

The paper reports a maximum accuracy of 99.90% but does not provide other standard classification metrics (e.g., sensitivity, specificity, precision, F1-score) or confidence intervals in the abstract, limiting the assessment of clinical robustness.
No information is provided regarding external validation, suggesting the model's generalizability to unseen clinical populations is unverified.

The study focuses on 'skin cancer' as a general category without specifying the histological subtypes (e.g., melanoma vs. benign lesions) included in the classification task, which is critical for clinical relevance.

The methodology combines multiple complex components (Wiener Filtering, U-Net, SqueezeNet, IWOA-optimized DNN) without clear justification for each step in the provided text, potentially introducing unnecessary complexity or data leakage risks if preprocessing steps were not strictly separated.
